refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, these are separated into distinct FRs focusing on what the system must do without implying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate an analysis output specifically describing the terrain characteristics of the worksite, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital model representing terrain features of the worksite
Extract and classify key site features relevant to planning and safety
Provide an interface to share the terrain model with planning and execution modules
Continuously update the terrain model to reflect changes during operations
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital model representing terrain features of the worksite

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model independently, which can then be used by other functions without direct dependencies, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of sufficient data to generate an accurate terrain model
Risk that the model may not capture all safety-relevant features if not properly specified
Assumes that the interface to planning/execution functions is well-defined elsewhere
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the data acquisition and model generation steps if data sources vary significantly, to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing and generating digital models of terrain features from sensor data and other inputs

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and environmental information needed to generate terrain models

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or requests to generate or update terrain models

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models to other system components or external users

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as terrain modeling is a core function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well defined through telemetry and network link.
Vehicle module primarily handles physical movement and control; terrain modeling would require significant diffusion outside this module.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring; terrain modeling is peripheral and would cause diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; terrain modeling unrelated and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of generating a digital terrain model, as it directly processes sensor data and environmental inputs to produce the model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may affect model accuracy.
If terrain modeling algorithms require frequent updates, changes may impact other NGHE functionalities.
Integration with other modules via network link must be carefully managed to avoid interface mismatches.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital model representing terrain features of the worksite' directly satisfies the FR 'provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by producing a digital representation that enables analysis. The DP clearly addresses the functional requirement and aligns with the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map for planning and execution.

dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (1). The module is responsible for processing sensor data and generating terrain models, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link). There is minimal risk of diffusion as terrain modeling is centralized in this module, avoiding scattering of responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by confirming that the NGHE Solution module produces the digital terrain model accessible to planning and execution functions, as per acceptance criteria. Interfaces allow input of sensor data and output of models for use by other system components.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined boundaries and interfaces supporting ownership and verification.

recommended_revisions: Monitor dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness to ensure model accuracy.
Establish clear interface contracts on the Network Link to prevent integration issues.
Plan for modular updates to terrain modeling algorithms to minimize impact on other NGHE functionalities.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear containment, alignment, and verification feasibility. The DP fully satisfies the FR by generating a digital terrain model enabling analysis, and the IM module NGHE Solution is well-contained with minimal diffusion risk. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable, with recommended revisions focusing on monitoring and interface management rather than further decomposition. Therefore, no further sub-FRs derivation is necessary at this time.

revise.why: To monitor dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness to ensure model accuracy, and to establish clear interface contracts on the Network Link to prevent integration issues, as well as plan for modular updates to terrain modeling algorithms.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can reliably process sensor data to generate accurate terrain models.
Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) are stable and well-defined.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data quality and timeliness may affect model accuracy.
Potential integration issues if interface contracts are not strictly enforced.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the NGHE Solution module produces accurate digital terrain models.
Interface test results ensuring compliance with defined contracts.
Monitoring data on telemetry data quality and its impact on model accuracy.
